tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Webhooks are the events that are sent to endpoints. Webhooks are sent to endpoints that are subscribed to the event type.

tags.name: Endpoints
tags.description: Endpoints are the destinations where events are sent. Endpoints can be subscribed to event types or events can be sent directly to an endpoint.

tags.name: Endpoint Groups
tags.description: Endpoint Groups are collections of endpoints that can be subscribed to event types or used to organize endpoints. Endpoints can be added to multiple endpoint groups. Endpoint groups can be used to represent all endpoints owned by a user, tenant, application, etc.

tags.name: Event Types
tags.description: Event Types are the types of events that can be sent to endpoints and can be used to specify schema to validate the payload of the event.

get.description: Get an endpoint group by ID

enabled.description: Whether the endpoint group is enabled

post.description:
Generate a portal URL/token for end user endpoint management. This generates a temporary link with full access to a specific endpoint group.

The portal can be themed by passing the `theme` query parameter to the portal URL returned in the response. Passing `theme=dark` or `theme=light` will set the theme and hide the theme toggle button. Passing `theme=default` will allow the user to toggle the theme (and will resest any previous restrictions).

When embedding the portal in an iframe, the `embed` query parameter can be passed to the portal URL returned in the response. Passing `embed=true` will render a limited version of the portal optimized for embedding.

API key requires the `endpoints:create`, `endpoints:read`, `endpoints:update`, and `endpoints:delete` permissions to access this endpoint.

allowedEventTypes.description: List of event types the user can subscribe to

applicationName.description: Name of the application for the back button

returnUrl.description: URL to return to when user clicks back button

portalUrl.description: URL to the portal with token

post.description:
Send an event to the specified endpoints.

 Events can be sent to endpoints and/or endpoint groups. 
 In order to send an event to an endpoint group, the eventType must be specified, then the event will be dispatched to all endpoints in the group that are subscribed to the eventType.

 API key requires the `webhooks:send` permission to access this endpoint.

destinations.description: List of endpoints or endpoint groups to send the event to

eventType.description: Event type to send the event to. Required if sending to an endpoint group.

payload.description: Payload to send with the event

eventId.description: Optional unique event ID to track the event
Tracking ID provided in the request

channels.description: Channels the webhook was sent to

400.description: Bad Request. Usually due to missing parameters, or invalid parameters.
